next_action: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting the Unity CI and build-validation policy for parallel human and AI-assisted production: local developer validation only; exhaustive full Windows player builds and all tests on every change; or a layered gate with fast deterministic edit-mode tests and content validation on ordinary branches, targeted play-mode tests for affected systems, a Windows player smoke build before integration, and scheduled or milestone full regression, performance, save-recovery, clean-machine, and artifact validation. Preserve Windows-first offline delivery, Unity with C#, plain-C# domain logic, modular content packages, generated registries, representative stress scenes, versioned snapshot and journal saves, accessibility, the stable 60 FPS target, and isolated branches. Include feedback speed, runner cost, flaky tests, Unity licensing or headless constraints, build reproducibility, artifact retention, branch protection, diagnostics, and the risks of both under-testing and making every small change wait for an exhaustive pipeline. Put the recommendation after all A/B/C options at the end of the card. Begin a new formal persistence batch D-221 through D-230 unless the user changes the cadence or requests a handoff.
